Annotations
Build

The worker delivers the block, then must answer >>CONFIRMED with per-AC evidence — the truth gate checks that commits exist, the trunk is untouched, and the engine's own test run is green before DONE stands.

Done?

The seat closes on >>DONE; the engine gate verifies verify_done + AC challenge — a bounce (capped at 2, then the operator) sends it back, never forward.

Review

A reviewer judges the delivery in its own detached checkout pinned to the engine-attested sha; APPROVED advances, REJECTED routes the findings back to the worker with the branch unchanged. Every verdict is recorded durably in reviews.md.

Approved?

>>APPROVED advances; >>REJECTED routes the findings back via the fix prompt (capped at 2). Every verdict is recorded in reviews.md.

Merge

The same worker owns the merge inside the single engine-wide window; the engine performs the mechanical land — fast-forward the ref, then re-run the full suite ON the trunk.

Merged?

The seat closes on >>MERGED; the engine gate verifies verify_merged — a bounce (capped at 2, then the operator) sends it back, never forward.

Land on · the trunk

Engine mechanical land: fast-forward the ref, then re-run the full suite ON the trunk — the claim is never trusted, the engine re-derives green itself.

Wrap

Still inside the window: the worker wraps — session log, docs, clean working tree. Only then does the arena retire. Done = landed + trunk-green + wrapped.

Wrapped?

The seat closes on >>WRAPPED; the engine gate verifies verify_wrapped — a bounce (capped at 2, then the operator) sends it back, never forward.

Land on · the trunk

Engine mechanical land: fast-forward the ref, then re-run the full suite ON the trunk — the claim is never trusted, the engine re-derives green itself.

Done

The register is stamped done — landed, trunk-green, and wrapped. Caps per phase: 2 gate bounces, 2 review rejects, 40 turns — each ends at the operator.

Seat stuck

A stuck seat raises the exception spine — a QUESTION, an engine wall, or an unparsable close.

Judge

Architect-first — the architect (an LLM) reads every stuck signal before anyone is paged: architect reads the block, rules or escalates; architect translates, rules, or escalates; architect rules first; a recurrence goes straight up; architect answers from the artifacts, or escalates.

Rule or escalate?

A ruling returns to the seat; an ESCALATE, a recurrence of an already-ruled wall, or the ablated architect_first arm sends the signal up to the operator.

Resolved

The ruling is relayed back to the stuck seat, which resumes.

Answer

The operator is the last resort and answers from anywhere via Telegram; the answer syncs back down through the architect.
